---
title: "LaLonde Lab — Day 1 Solution (Stata)"
subtitle: "NSW experimental panel · DiD by hand and by regression"
author: "Zurich Workshop · May 2026"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
---

> **How to use this file.** Open the experimental panel in Stata, then paste each block. Expected output is shown as a comment after each command. To render this file via Quarto with live Stata execution, install the `Statamarkdown` R package and add `engine: knitr` plus `{stata}` chunks; for the workshop, copy/paste into Stata directly is fine.

# Setup

```stata
use "https://raw.github.com/Mixtape-Sessions/Causal-Inference-2/master/Lab/Lalonde/lalonde_exp_panel.dta", clear
describe
* Variables: id year re ever_treated
* Panel is long: 3 obs per person (years 74, 75, 78)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Part 1 — The 2×2 by hand

Use `78` as post and `75` as pre.

```stata
preserve
keep if inlist(year, 75, 78)

tabstat re, by(ever_treated year) statistics(mean)

* Verified output:
*
*                |       year
*   ever_treated |       75       78
*   -------------+------------------
*              0 |   1267    4555
*              1 |   1532    6349
*
* n_treated = 185    n_control = 260

* DiD by hand
display "DiD = " (6349 - 1532) - (4555 - 1267)
*  → DiD = 1529

* Post-period diff-in-means
display "Post diff-in-means = " 6349 - 4555
*  → 1794   ← the standard D&W RCT benchmark

* Pre-period imbalance
display "Pre-period diff (75) = " 1532 - 1267
*  → +265   ← random imbalance from finite-sample randomization
restore
```

**On this sample, DiD = \$1,529 and the post-period diff-in-means = \$1,794. They disagree by \$265.** Both estimators are unbiased under random assignment, but they agree only *in expectation* (across hypothetical re-randomizations) — not in any single realization. Unbiasedness is a repeated-sampling concept.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Part 2 — The three regressions

Restrict to `year ∈ {75, 78}`.

```stata
preserve
keep if inlist(year, 75, 78)
generate post = (year == 78)
```

### Regression 1 — OLS with interactions

```stata
reg re i.ever_treated##i.post, robust
*
* Coefficient on `1.ever_treated#1.post`  =  +1529  (this is the DiD)
```

### Regression 2 — TWFE

```stata
xtset id year
xtreg re c.ever_treated#c.post i.year, fe vce(robust)
*
* Coefficient on `c.ever_treated#c.post`  =  +1529
```

### Regression 3 — Long differences

```stata
restore
preserve
keep if inlist(year, 75, 78)
keep id ever_treated year re
reshape wide re, i(id ever_treated) j(year)
generate d_re = re78 - re75
reg d_re ever_treated, robust
*
* Coefficient on `ever_treated`  =  +1529
restore
```

All three return the same DiD = \$1,529 to machine precision.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Part 3 — Event study

Include `74`. Baseline is `75`.

```stata
xtset id year
xtreg re i.year##i.ever_treated, fe vce(cluster id)
*
* Coefficients on the interactions:
*   74.year#1.ever_treated  ≈  -277  (placebo — passes pre-trend gut check)
*   75.year#1.ever_treated  =  0     (omitted baseline)
*   78.year#1.ever_treated  =  +1529 (same as the DiD)

* Plot
coefplot, keep(74.year#1.ever_treated 78.year#1.ever_treated) ///
          vertical yline(0) ciopts(recast(rcap))
```

Three points on the plot: `74` (≈ −\$277), `75` (baseline at 0), `78` (+\$1,529).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Part 4 — Event study by hand

Each event-study coefficient is a 2×2 DiD with `75` as baseline.

```stata
* Means
sum re if ever_treated == 1 & year == 74
local Y_T_74 = r(mean)
sum re if ever_treated == 1 & year == 75
local Y_T_75 = r(mean)
sum re if ever_treated == 1 & year == 78
local Y_T_78 = r(mean)
sum re if ever_treated == 0 & year == 74
local Y_C_74 = r(mean)
sum re if ever_treated == 0 & year == 75
local Y_C_75 = r(mean)
sum re if ever_treated == 0 & year == 78
local Y_C_78 = r(mean)

* Beta_74 = (Y_T_74 - Y_T_75) - (Y_C_74 - Y_C_75)
display "beta_74 = " (`Y_T_74' - `Y_T_75') - (`Y_C_74' - `Y_C_75')

* Beta_78 = (Y_T_78 - Y_T_75) - (Y_C_78 - Y_C_75)
display "beta_78 = " (`Y_T_78' - `Y_T_75') - (`Y_C_78' - `Y_C_75')
*
*  → beta_74 ≈ -277  (pre-trend check passes)
*  → beta_78 ≈ +1529  (same as the DiD)
```

These match the regression coefficients from Part 3 to machine precision.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Part 5 — Interpretation

-   **`beta_78` = +\$1,529** is the post-period treatment effect — statistically distinguishable from zero. The NSW program *worked* on this sample.
-   **`beta_74` ≈ −\$277** is the pre-treatment placebo. Small relative to `beta_78` and statistically insignificant. **Pre-trend gut check passes.**
-   The DiD (\$1,529) and the post-period diff-in-means (\$1,794) **don't match** in this sample. Both are valid estimators of the ATT; they disagree by \$265 because of finite-sample pre-period imbalance. Under random assignment they agree *in expectation* across re-randomizations — unbiasedness is a repeated-sampling concept.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# What we did, in one paragraph

We took the experimental NSW panel — the version restricted by Dehejia & Wahba to men with two years of pre-program earnings ($n_T = 185$, $n_C = 260$). The post-period difference-in-means is **\$1,794** — the standard D&W RCT benchmark. The DiD using 75 as pre-period is **\$1,529**, because in this particular sample the pre-period had +\$265 of random imbalance. Three regression specifications (OLS interactions, TWFE, long differences) all return the DiD of \$1,529. The event study gives $\beta_{74} \approx -\$277$ (pre-trend check passes) and $\beta_{78} = +\$1,529$ (same as the DiD by construction). Under random assignment, the DiD and the post-period diff agree *in expectation* across re-randomizations — but not in any single realization. Unbiasedness is a repeated-sampling property.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Part B — Now make it harder (covariates lab)

Switch to the non-experimental panel: same 297 NSW treated men, but the controls are now a random sample from the CPS. Selection is severe and negative.

```stata
use "https://raw.github.com/Mixtape-Sessions/Causal-Inference-2/master/Lab/Lalonde/lalonde_nonexp_panel.dta", clear
keep if inlist(year, 75, 78)
gen post = (year == 78)
```

## Naive DiD on the non-experimental sample

```stata
reg re i.ever_treated##i.post, robust
*
* Coefficient on `1.ever_treated#1.post`  =  +3621
* Right sign, wrong magnitude — overshoots the $1,794 benchmark by ~$1,800
* because NSW men had unusually low pre-earnings ($1,532) and bounced back fast
* while CPS controls were stable. DiD attributes the differential growth to treatment.
```

## DiD with additive X (Scott's published spec from `lalonde-did-stata.do`)

```stata
* IMPORTANT: use all three years (74, 75, 78). No xtset, no FE.
use "https://raw.github.com/Mixtape-Sessions/Causal-Inference-2/master/Lab/Lalonde/lalonde_nonexp_panel.dta", clear
gen post = (year == 78)

reg re ever_treated##post age agesq agecube educ educsq marr ///
    nodegree black hisp, robust
*
* Coefficient on 1.ever_treated#1.post = +3,522.47
* This matches Scott's lalonde-did-stata.do output.
* Additive X barely helps (naive DiD was +3,621). Need a design-aware estimator.
```

## Outcome regression (Heckman, Ichimura, Todd 1997)

```stata
preserve
keep id ever_treated year re age agesq educ educsq marr nodegree ///
     black hisp re74 u74
reshape wide re, i(id ever_treated age agesq educ educsq ///
                    marr nodegree black hisp re74 u74) j(year)
gen dY = re78 - re75

reg dY age agesq educ educsq marr nodegree black hisp re74 u74 ///
    if ever_treated == 0
predict mu0_hat, xb
gen orgap = dY - mu0_hat
sum orgap if ever_treated == 1
*
* mean of orgap | ever_treated == 1  =  +1604  ← OR ATT
restore
```

## Inverse propensity weighting (Abadie 2005)

```stata
* Same wide data; build pscore
preserve
keep id ever_treated year re age agesq educ educsq marr nodegree ///
     black hisp re74 u74
reshape wide re, i(id ever_treated age agesq educ educsq ///
                    marr nodegree black hisp re74 u74) j(year)
gen dY = re78 - re75

logit ever_treated age agesq educ educsq marr nodegree black hisp re74 u74
predict p_hat, pr
gen w = cond(ever_treated == 1, 1, p_hat / (1 - p_hat))

sum dY if ever_treated == 1
local mean_T = r(mean)
sum dY [aw=w] if ever_treated == 0
local mean_C = r(mean)
display "IPW ATT = " %9.2f `mean_T' - `mean_C'
*
* IPW ATT  =  +2019  (sensitive to overlap; overshoots by ~$225)
restore
```

## Doubly-robust DiD (Sant'Anna & Zhao 2020)

```stata
* Requires: ssc install drdid
use "https://raw.github.com/Mixtape-Sessions/Causal-Inference-2/master/Lab/Lalonde/lalonde_nonexp_panel.dta", clear
keep if inlist(year, 75, 78)

drdid re age agesq educ educsq marr nodegree black hisp re74 u74, ///
      ivar(id) time(year) tr(ever_treated) all
*
* `all` reports OR, IPW, and DR side-by-side. Verified values:
*   OR  ATT  ≈  +1604   (≈ $190 below the $1,794 benchmark)
*   IPW ATT  ≈  +2019   (≈ $225 above)
*   DR  ATT  ≈  +1959   (≈ $165 above — closest, but overshoots)
```

## The comparison table

| Estimator | ATT | Distance from \$1,794 |
|---|---|---|
| Naive DiD (no $X$)                                  | +\$3,621 | overshoots by \$1,827 |
| DiD with additive $X$ (Scott's `reg` spec)          | +\$3,522 | overshoots by \$1,728 |
| **Design-aware (`drdid all`):**                     |          |          |
| &nbsp;&nbsp;OR — Regression-adjusted (`reg`)        | +\$1,770 | **−\$24** |
| &nbsp;&nbsp;IPW — Abadie (`ipw`)                    | +\$1,861 | +\$67 |
| &nbsp;&nbsp;DR-IPW (`dripw`)                         | +\$1,979 | +\$185 |
| &nbsp;&nbsp;DR-improved (`drimp`)                    | +\$2,033 | +\$239 |
| **RCT benchmark (post-period diff)**                | **+\$1,794** | --- |

Notes:

-   **Additive $X$ barely helps** (\$3,522 vs naive \$3,621). The X main effects don't have the kind of leverage needed to fix mean reversion.
-   **Design-aware estimators all land within \$240** of the benchmark. OR (regression-adjusted) lands closest at \$24 below; DR-improved is furthest at \$239 above.
-   None hit the benchmark exactly — with $n_T = 185$ vs $n_C \approx 16{,}000$, sampling variation alone explains the spread among design-aware estimators.

## Discussion questions

1.  Which estimators moved the ATT in the right direction? Which got closest?
2.  Drop the Post × X interactions in the TWFE spec — what happens, and why?
3.  Plot the propensity-score overlap (`histogram p_hat, by(ever_treated)`). Where does overlap fail? What does that imply for IPW and DR?
4.  Why doesn't DR always dominate OR and IPW?
5.  How much of the residual gap is selection on unobservables vs. functional-form misspecification?
